question_text: For x ∈R, the ﬂoor function is denoted by f(x) = ⌊x⌋and deﬁned as follows ⌊x⌋= k, k ≤x < k + 1, where k is an integer. Let Y = ⌊X⌋, where X is an exponentially distributed random variable with mean ln 10, where ln denotes natural logarithm. For any positive integer ℓ, one can write the probability of the event Y = ℓas follows P(Y = ℓ) = qℓ(1 −q) The value of q is
